rationale: A cmd.exe process (PID 2936) running as corp\Administrator at High integrity level orchestrated four sequential curl.exe invocations (A0, EV-002, EV-004, EV-006) to download a DLL from a raw GitHub commit URL using -k (TLS verification bypass), depositing confirmed copies to three separate writable locations: C:\Users\Public\Music\ (EV-001, EV-003), C:\ProgramData\ (EV-005), and the user's Temp directory (EV-007). The redundant multi-path download pattern — attempting the same payload to multiple staging locations in rapid succession — is a classic resilience/fallback technique used during malware staging to ensure at least one copy survives. The use of -k to bypass certificate validation, the raw GitHub commit URL pointing to a specific commit hash (indicating deliberate pinning of a payload version), the DLL file type dropped to world-writable directories, and the high-integrity Administrator context all strongly indicate malicious payload staging. No evidence of subsequent DLL execution or loading is present in this package, but confirmed file writes to multiple locations are sufficient to classify this as malicious.
